title: Comms Datalink Encryption Upgrade Review — 2025-01-22
text:
# Comms Datalink Encryption Upgrade Review — 2025-01-22

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Technical Design Review  
**Attendees:** Priya Nair (Comms), Sam Dreher (GNC), Felix Herrera (Integration), Jordan Kim (GCS SW), Tyler Moss (Firmware), Nina Schulz (Quality)  
**Chair:** Priya Nair  
**Minutes recorded by:** Jordan Kim

---

## Background

A penetration test conducted by an external security assessment firm in December 2024 (report ref: MD-SEC-2024-11) identified that the K-200 datalink using the original FreeWave P-1125 radio with no end-to-end encryption was vulnerable to command injection — an attacker with a compatible radio could inject spoofed command packets. This was escalated as KES-201 (epic: comms encryption upgrade). The customer's security addendum to the contract (ref: KES-203) requires AES-256-GCM encryption on all command and telemetry traffic by programme milestone M4 (March 2025).

## Architecture Proposal

Priya Nair presented the encryption architecture:

1. **FPGA encryption module (P-1127):** A Lattice iCE40-based FPGA module implementing AES-256-GCM in hardware sits between the datalink controller (P-1121) and the radio (P-1125/P-1126). All command/telemetry data is encrypted before transmission and decrypted on receipt.
2. **Key management:** Session keys derived via ECDH on each power-cycle, using a pre-shared master key stored in secure flash on P-1127. Key rotation every 60 minutes of continuous operation.
3. **Bandwidth impact:** AES-256-GCM adds 16-byte authentication tag per packet and ~3% throughput overhead. This was the primary driver for upgrading the radio from P-1125 (500kbps) to P-1126 (2Mbps), maintaining adequate headroom for telemetry at the higher overhead.

Jordan Kim confirmed that the GCS software (SRC-024 gcs_ui_backend.py) will require a protocol stack update to handle encrypted sessions, tracked as KES-203.

Tyler Moss confirmed that encryption_handler.c (SRC-018) implementing the FPGA interface is complete and passing unit tests.

## Decision

**AGREED:** Implement AES-256-GCM encryption via P-1127 FPGA module. Upgrade radio to P-1126 to accommodate bandwidth overhead.

- Decision maker: Priya Nair (Comms lead), approved by Felix Herrera (Systems Integration)
- Rationale: Customer contract requirement (KES-203), penetration test finding (MD-SEC-2024-11), bandwidth headroom needed for encrypted telemetry
- ECR to be raised: ECR-229 (Priya Nair, covering P-1125 → P-1126 swap and P-1127 addition)
- Documents to update: DOC-471 (Comms ICD), DOC-472 (Comms Protocol Spec), SRC-018 (encryption_handler.c)
- GCS update: SRC-024 to implement encrypted session protocol (Jordan Kim, by 2025-02-28)

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-229 | Priya Nair | 2025-01-29 |
| A2 | Complete SRC-018 encryption_handler.c integration testing | Tyler Moss | 2025-02-14 |
| A3 | Update DOC-471 Comms ICD to Rev C | Priya Nair | 2025-02-28 |
| A4 | Update DOC-472 Protocol Spec to Rev B | Priya Nair | 2025-02-28 |
| A5 | Update GCS SRC-024 for encrypted session protocol | Jordan Kim | 2025-02-28 |
| A6 | Update DRW-531 antenna mount for P-1126 | Rowan Ellery | 2025-02-15 |
